originalAlarm.description: Memory dynamically allocated by `malloc` on line 1576 is not freed after the last access at line 1576, column 2.

sanitizedDescription: Memory dynamically allocated by `malloc` on line 1576 is not freed after the last access at line 1576, column 2.
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  true)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __malloc_and_calloc_defined () Bool
  false)
reasoning: eckey is allocated by malloc at L10 when HAVE_ECC=T, but is never freed before return 0 at L24 — genuine memory leak. The comment at L22-23 confirms the intent ("return having forgotten to free the memory"). No // ERROR in the file; new bug. PC = !_STDLIB_H AND __need_malloc_and_calloc AND !__malloc_and_calloc_defined AND HAVE_ECC: real feature flag is HAVE_ECC; the three library macros (!_STDLIB_H, __need_malloc_and_calloc, !__malloc_and_calloc_defined) are all restrictive (non-standard stdlib.h processing state). PC is over-constrained — bug fires in all HAVE_ECC=T configs, not just this library header state.
